title: Top 25 Forward Deployed Engineer Voices of 2026
subtitle: The most influential FDEs, customer engineers, and field engineers shaping the profession
methodology: Ranked by cross-list appearances, public writing, community impact, and relevance to Forward Deployed Engineering. Sources include Pragmatic Engineer, SVPG, company engineering blogs (Palantir, OpenAI, Anthropic, Scale AI), and independent analysis. We evaluated 40+ candidates and filtered for people who actively shape how FDE teams operate.

voices.name: Gergely Orosz
voices.title: Author & Newsletter Writer
voices.bio: Runs The Pragmatic Engineer newsletter (700K+ subscribers) and wrote the definitive coverage of the FDE role. His analysis of how companies like Palantir, OpenAI, and Scale AI deploy engineers directly to customers shaped how the industry understands and values the role.

voices.name: Marty Cagan
voices.title: Author & Partner
voices.bio: Author of 'Inspired,' 'Empowered,' and 'Transformed.' SVPG's coverage of the FDE model as a product delivery pattern helped legitimize the role beyond Palantir. His frameworks for embedded engineering teams influence how companies think about customer-facing technical roles.

voices.name: Shyam Sankar
voices.title: CTO
voices.bio: Palantir's CTO and the architect of the original Forward Deployed Engineering model. Palantir invented the FDE role, and Sankar's vision of engineers embedded with customers solving their hardest problems became the template that every AI company now copies.

voices.name: Alexandr Wang
voices.title: CEO & Founder
voices.bio: Built Scale AI into one of the largest employers of Forward Deployed Engineers outside of Palantir. Scale's FDE program, which deploys engineers to help enterprise customers implement AI at production scale, became the blueprint for the AI-era FDE role.

voices.name: Kevin Hartz
voices.title: Partner
voices.bio: A16z partner who has written and spoken about the FDE model as a competitive advantage for AI companies. His investment thesis on why FDEs create defensible moats influenced how startups think about customer-embedded engineering.

voices.name: Ali Ghodsi
voices.title: CEO & Co-Founder
voices.bio: Built Databricks' field engineering organization into one of the strongest customer-embedded technical teams in enterprise software. Databricks' approach to deploying engineers alongside customers during implementation became a model for the data/AI infrastructure space.

voices.name: Clément Delangue
voices.title: CEO & Co-Founder
voices.bio: Hugging Face's CEO who built a model where community engineers serve as de facto FDEs, helping enterprise customers deploy open-source AI models. His approach to customer-embedded support through open source is a new flavor of forward deployment.

voices.name: Will Larson
voices.title: CTO & Author
voices.bio: Author of 'Staff Engineer' and 'An Elegant Puzzle.' His writing on engineering career ladders, staff+ roles, and organizational design shapes how FDEs think about their career trajectory. The concept of 'staff engineer as force multiplier' maps directly to the FDE value proposition.

voices.name: Chip Huyen
voices.title: Author & ML Engineer
voices.bio: Author of 'Designing Machine Learning Systems.' Her work on ML deployment, real-world ML challenges, and the gap between ML research and production systems speaks directly to the FDE mission of making AI work in the field, not just in the lab.

voices.name: Hamel Husain
voices.title: ML Engineer & Educator
voices.bio: Former ML engineer at GitHub and Airbnb. His blog and teaching on practical ML engineering, LLM fine-tuning, and customer-facing AI implementation make him one of the most followed voices for engineers who deploy AI in production environments.

voices.name: Swyx (Shawn Wang)
voices.title: Founder & AI Engineer
voices.bio: Runs the Latent Space podcast and newsletter covering AI engineering. His concept of 'AI Engineer' as a new role category overlaps heavily with the FDE role. His content on building AI applications for real users, not just demos, resonates with FDEs.

voices.name: Eugene Yan
voices.title: Senior Applied Scientist
voices.bio: Applied scientist at Amazon who writes extensively about deploying ML systems in production. His blog on recommendation systems, LLM applications, and the gap between research and deployment is a masterclass in what FDEs deal with daily.

voices.name: Lenny Rachitsky
voices.title: Newsletter Author
voices.bio: 500K+ subscribers. His coverage of how product teams work with customer-facing engineers, and his analysis of companies that deploy engineers directly to customers, helps FDEs understand how their role fits into the broader product organization.

voices.name: Jason Warner
voices.title: Managing Director
voices.bio: Former CTO of GitHub. Now at Redpoint where he invests in infrastructure and developer tools companies that rely on FDE-style deployment models. His perspective on when companies need FDEs vs. traditional support/implementation teams is unique and valuable.

voices.name: Shreya Shankar
voices.title: ML Engineer & PhD Researcher
voices.bio: PhD researcher at Berkeley working on ML deployment and data quality. Her papers on real-world ML challenges and her open-source tools for production ML systems address the exact problems FDEs solve when they're embedded with customers.

voices.name: Tao An
voices.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
voices.bio: Palantir FDE alumni who wrote one of the most-read Medium posts on what the FDE role actually looks like day to day. His candid description of embedded engineering work, on-site rotations, and customer problem-solving gave the industry its clearest picture of the role.

voices.name: Vicki Boykis
voices.title: Senior ML Engineer
voices.bio: Senior ML engineer at Mozilla AI. Her newsletter 'Normcore Tech' and blog cover the messy reality of deploying ML systems. Her writing on data engineering, recommendation systems, and production ML challenges speaks to the FDE experience.

voices.name: Zach Mueller
voices.title: ML Engineer
voices.bio: ML engineer at Hugging Face who works directly with enterprise customers deploying open-source models. His practical content on fine-tuning, model deployment, and making AI work in real environments is what the FDE role looks like in the open-source ecosystem.

voices.name: Nikhil Krishnan
voices.title: Founder
voices.bio: Runs the Out of Pocket newsletter covering healthcare tech. His analysis of how healthcare AI companies deploy FDEs to work with hospitals and health systems shows where the FDE model is expanding beyond traditional tech sectors.

voices.name: Itamar Friedman
voices.title: CEO & Co-Founder
voices.bio: Built Codium AI with an FDE-first go-to-market approach, deploying engineers directly to enterprise customers during onboarding. His model of using FDEs as the primary sales motion for developer tools is being adopted across the AI developer tools space.

voices.name: Andrew Trask
voices.title: Research Scientist & Founder
voices.bio: Research scientist at DeepMind and founder of OpenMined. His work on privacy-preserving AI and federated learning requires deep customer-embedded deployment, making him a voice on how FDEs operate in highly regulated, data-sensitive environments.

voices.name: Daniel Firu
voices.title: Head of Forward Deployed Engineering
voices.bio: Leads FDE teams at Palantir, the company that invented the role. His perspective on what makes FDEs succeed, how to build FDE organizations, and how the role has evolved from Palantir's early days to the current AI era comes from the source.

voices.name: Sarah Catanzaro
voices.title: Partner
voices.bio: VC at Amplify Partners who invests in data and AI infrastructure. Her analysis of the FDE model as a go-to-market strategy and her writing on customer-embedded engineering teams influence how startups decide whether to build FDE organizations.

voices.name: Peter Welinder
voices.title: VP of Product & Partnerships
voices.bio: Leads product and partnerships at OpenAI, overseeing how OpenAI deploys engineers to work with enterprise customers integrating GPT-4 and other models. OpenAI's FDE program is the most sought-after in the industry right now.

voices.name: Tim Chen
voices.title: VP of Engineering
voices.bio: Leads engineering at Ramp, where customer-embedded engineers help enterprise customers integrate financial automation. Ramp's rapid growth and technical complexity make their engineering org a case study in how FDE-style roles work outside pure AI companies.
